mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month growth of RSAFS over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which typically precedes an economic slowdown and reduced corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an accommodative monetary environment, reducing the cost of capital and boosting corporate valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (within +/- 0.5%) in more than 50% of periods where both CPIAUCSL YoY growth is below 2% and FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, a sudden deceleration signals potential supply chain disruptions or weakening demand for goods, which can negatively impact corporate earnings and stock market sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 1-month change turns negative after 3 consecutive months of positive growth
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are predominantly positive (more than 55% of triggered instances) when INDPRO growth decelerates after a period of expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates a weakening in the construction sector and broader economic activity, signaling a slowdown in consumer spending and investment, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average falls by more than 10% from its 12-month peak
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods following a significant decline in housing starts as defined by the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained strong payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy labor market, supporting consumer income and spending, which in turn drives corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where PAYEMS shows strong average growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden, sharp cut in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) by the central bank signals an emergency response to economic distress, which, despite the easing, often indicates severe underlying problems that will negatively impact market sentiment in the very short term, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a sharp cut in the federal funds rate.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining retail sales (RSAFS) and falling industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a broad-based contraction in both consumer demand and manufacturing output, signaling a significant economic downturn that will lead to sustained negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth < 0% AND INDPRO 3-month average growth < 0%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of periods where both retail sales and industrial production are contracting over a three-month average.
